_comment: Maps security platform names to the ATT&CK for ICS technique IDs they can detect. Based on public vendor docs, ATT&CK evaluations, and capability disclosures as of early 2025. Edit this file to reflect your actual deployed stack — the gap analysis is only as good as what you put in here.

claroty.description: Claroty xDome / Continuous Threat Detection. Strong OT protocol inspection and network anomaly detection. Limited on endpoint/host-based techniques since it's network-passive.

dragos.description: Dragos Platform. Best-in-class ICS threat hunting and protocol analysis. Has named threat group detection content for CHERNOVITE, ELECTRUM, VOLTZITE, KAMACITE, and others. Weakest on pure endpoint techniques.

nozomi.description: Nozomi Networks Guardian / Arc. Solid OT protocol breadth and asset visibility. Relative strength in wireless and serial protocol detection. Less threat-group-specific content than Dragos.

splunk.description: Splunk Enterprise Security with the ICS/SCADA add-on and relevant data sources (Sysmon, WEF, firewall, historian logs). Coverage heavily depends on what you're actually ingesting — this reflects a well-tuned deployment.

qradar.description: IBM QRadar SIEM with ICS/SCADA content extensions. Similar log-source coverage to Splunk but fewer community-built ICS-specific rules. Assumes Guardium or network flow data is feeding the SIEM.

sentinel.description: Microsoft Sentinel with Defender for IoT integration. Unusually broad for a SIEM because Defender for IoT feeds OT-specific alerts directly into the workspace. Best SIEM option for environments that already run Microsoft.

crowdstrike.description: CrowdStrike Falcon (EDR/XDR). Excellent on Windows/Linux endpoint techniques — process, persistence, privilege escalation, credential access. Network visibility through Falcon Insight. Blind to OT-layer protocol techniques since it doesn't speak Modbus or DNP3.

defender.description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint + Defender for IoT combined. Strong Windows endpoint coverage. When Defender for IoT sensors are deployed you also get some OT-layer visibility — a meaningful advantage over most EDRs in OT environments.

zeek.description: Zeek (formerly Bro) network analysis framework with ICSNPP ICS protocol scripts. Pure network visibility — very strong on traffic-based detection but zero host/endpoint telemetry. Coverage assumes the ICSNPP plugin suite (Modbus, DNP3, EtherNet/IP, BACnet, etc.) is installed.

snort.description: Snort 3 IDS/IPS with community + Talos ICS rulesets. Signature-based so it only catches what it has rules for. Good on known exploit patterns and malformed protocol packets; poor on behavioural / anomaly techniques. Narrower than Zeek for OT.
